calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-12-2026 Set For Trial On May-04-27 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-02-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
John Roach, Esq. 415-851-4557 Law Office for Plaintiff.
Kat Todd, Esq. 415-366-5543 Sophia Aulicino, Esq. 805-433-2575 J. Supple Law for Defendant.
Court reporter: Nancy Presant, CSR 906, 510-757-8550, npresantcsr@gmail.com

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED.

Ms. Todd contests and presents arguments; complaint (specifically paragraphs 16 and 19 conclusory and don't state grounds for a bystander cause of action, need to prove contemporaneous awareness, cites Bird v Saenz, oversimplifying, professional negligence not caretaking, should prove breach, cites Downey v Riverside case.
The Court comments; overthinking and overcomplicating, nature of negligence, need to allege specific negligence hospital worker committed and specific breach.
Mr. Roach responds; concedes 2nd element at issue, contemporaneous negligence, TR got it right, Bird case confined to invisible technical error, nothing hidden in falling, readily observable, suffered great emotional distress.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant The Regents of the University of California's demurrer to Plaintiffs Flora Lau, Ashley Lau, and Joshua Lau's complaint.

Taken Under Submission.***

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Nancy Presant, CSR 906, 510-757-8550, npresantcsr@gmail.com, Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing, the court signs an order. (see scanned order for details)***

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-30-2026 Off Calendar
